title: Travel & Transportation
description: Book trips, navigate airports and stations, and handle travel situations with confidence.

items.example: I booked my flight three months in advance.

items.back: billete de ida / ida y vuelta
items.example: A round-trip ticket is cheaper if you stay over a Saturday.
Departure is at 08:00 and arrival is at 11:30.
Your flight leaves from gate 22 in terminal B.

items.front: to miss the bus / train / flight
items.example: We missed our flight because of the traffic jam.
Please check in online at least 24 hours before boarding.
We waited 45 minutes at passport control.
There are no direct flights — you'll have a 2-hour layover in Madrid.
The flight is delayed by two hours due to bad weather.

items.prompt: I'd like a ___ ticket to London, please. I'm coming back on Sunday.
items.explanation: A 'return' ticket (UK English) = round-trip. Coming back confirms you need both directions.

items.prompt: Hurry! If we don't run, we'll ___ the last train.
items.explanation: 'Miss' is the correct verb for transport: miss the bus, miss the flight.

items.prompt: Please have your passport ready for ___ before entering the country.
items.explanation: 'Passport control' = the official check of passports at borders. Customs deals with goods.

items.prompt: Flight BA472 has been ___. All passengers will be rebooked.
items.explanation: 'Cancelled' = the flight will not operate at all. 'Delayed' just means it's late.

items.prompt: The train to Manchester departs from ___ 7.
items.explanation: Trains depart from 'platforms'. Gates are used in airports.

items.sentence: I ___ my flight because the taxi was late. I had to book a new one.
Your flight ___ from gate 14B. Please board 30 minutes before departure.
Is this a ___ flight or do I have a stopover somewhere?
Please ___ in at least two hours before your international flight.
The flight is ___ by one hour due to a technical issue.
